tasks.title: Printing Your Name
tasks.explainer:
When we want to print to the screen we use `python:print()` - Anything inside the brackets will be printed to the console! 

But we also need to put our message inside **quotation marks** `python:print(" ")`

Try it below, put *between* the quotation marks your name: 
```python
print("Your name")
```

tasks.title: Printing Hello World
tasks.explainer:
Can you write a `python:print()` command from scratch? 

Try writing out the code from below: 

```python
print("Hello world")
```

tasks.title: Printing Multiple Lines
tasks.explainer:
What about if we want to print out multiple lines? 

We can add another line and write another `python:print()` command! This will be printed out in the console on the next line. 

```python
print("Hello world")
print("My name is Jeff")
```
tasks.completeCode:
print("Hello world")
print("My name is Ryan")

tasks.title: Debugging Errors
tasks.explainer:
What about if we write our code wrong? 

An error message will show in the output. Try running the code below and seeing the error. Then see if you can fix it!

**Hint:** Our message inside our print should be between quotation marks! `python:print(" ")`

tasks.title: Ascii Art Challenge - 1
tasks.explainer:
Ascii art is using letters and numbers to draw a picture! 

Can you use what you have learned so far to modify what we have to print out a message to the output that looks like this: 

```
****
****
****
****
```

tasks.title: Ascii Art Challenge - 2
tasks.explainer:
Can you change it to print out this arrow drawing? 
```
  *
 ***
*****
  *
  *
```
tasks.completeCode:
print("  *")
print(" ***")
print("*****")
print("  *")
print("  *")
print("  *")

tasks.title: Ascii Art Challenge - 3
tasks.explainer:
What about this smily face? 

```
  00000
 0     0
0  0 0  0
0       0
0   U   0
 0     0
  00000
```
tasks.starterCode:
print("  *")
print(" ***")
print("*****")
print("  *")
print("  *")
print("  *")
tasks.completeCode:
print("  00000")
print(" 0     0")
print("0  0 0  0")
print("0       0")
print("0   U   0")
print(" 0     0")
print("  00000")
